message:
feat(ffs-repair): implement scrub pipeline for corruption detection (bd-b80)

Adds a format-agnostic block scrub engine that iterates over device blocks,
validates each via a pluggable BlockValidator trait, and collects structured
findings into a ScrubReport.

Key types:
- CorruptionKind: checksum mismatch, structural invariant, bad magic, zeroes, I/O
- Severity: info/warning/error/critical with Ord for filtering
- ScrubFinding: per-block finding with kind, severity, and detail
- ScrubReport: aggregate with blocks_scanned/corrupt/io_error counts
- BlockValidator trait: pluggable validation (ext4/btrfs implement their own)
- Scrubber: scan engine with scrub_range() and scrub_all()
- ZeroCheckValidator and CompositeValidator built-in implementations

12 tests verify: clean reports, bit-flip detection (deterministic), multiple
corrupt blocks, I/O error recording, range clamping, zero-check, composite
validation, severity ordering, display formatting, and no-panic on garbage data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
